name: Firmware Modification
description: Changes made to firmware, which may include its settings, configurations, or underlying data. This can encompass alterations to the Master Boot Record (MBR), Volume Boot Record (VBR), or other firmware components critical to system boot and functionality. Such modifications are often indicators of adversary activity, including malware persistence and system compromise.

modification_scenarios.description: Modifying the MBR to load malicious code during the boot process.
Altering the VBR to redirect boot processes to malicious locations.
Updating firmware with a malicious or unauthorized image.
Logs showing unauthorized firmware updates or checksum mismatches.

bios_uefi_logs.action: Enable and monitor BIOS/UEFI logs to capture settings changes or firmware updates.

firmware_integrity_monitoring.action: Use tools or firmware security features to detect changes to firmware components.

endpoint_detection_response.action: Detect abnormal firmware activity, such as changes to MBR/VBR or unauthorized firmware updates.

events.description: Raw disk access (indicates potential MBR/VBR manipulation).

linux.description: Monitor write/attribute changes to the raw disk device.

network_traffic_analysis.action: Capture firmware updates downloaded over the network, particularly from untrusted sources.

secure_boot_logs.action: Collect and analyze Secure Boot logs for signs of tampering.

log_sources.Name: auditd:SYSCALL
log_sources.Channel: ioctl/write: Direct firmware update or device memory manipulation syscalls

log_sources.Name: etw:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Storage
log_sources.Channel: Raw disk I/O operations bypassing NTFS APIs

log_sources.Name: firmware:integrity
log_sources.Channel: Baseline mismatch or unexpected EFI module detected during integrity checks

log_sources.Name: firmware:integrity
log_sources.Channel: Firmware integrity verification failures or mismatches against expected UEFI/firmware image baselines

log_sources.Name: firmware:runtime
log_sources.Channel: Debug or memory access commands indicating attempts to alter OS instructions in memory

log_sources.Name: firmware:smart
log_sources.Channel: Unexpected firmware-level errors or abnormal S.M.A.R.T. log entries

log_sources.Name: macos:osquery
log_sources.Channel: Unexpected changes in EFI or NVRAM variables controlling hardware boot state

log_sources.Name: macos:unifiedlog
log_sources.Channel: boot failure events or SMC validation errors

log_sources.Name: macos:unifiedlog
log_sources.Channel: Firmware update events or kernel extension (kext) loads not signed by Apple

log_sources.Name: networkdevice:config
log_sources.Channel: Boot image path or firmware configuration variable modified outside of maintenance windows

log_sources.Name: networkdevice:config
log_sources.Channel: Log entries indicating ROMMON image upgrade commands (boot system, upgrade rom-monitor)

log_sources.Name: networkdevice:config
log_sources.Channel: Boot variable modified to point to non-standard or unsigned image

log_sources.Name: networkdevice:firmware
log_sources.Channel: Firmware update initiated or bootloader tampering detected

log_sources.Name: networkdevice:syslog
log_sources.Channel: Boot information log showing image loaded from TFTP server instead of local storage

log_sources.Name: WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Boot
log_sources.Channel: Firmware integrity validation failed or boot configuration tampered
